ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301724 represents the cola soft drink category; no exact entry for Coca-Cola Original Taste brand product exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers cola-type soft drinks as a category. No brand-specific Coca-Cola Original Taste code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 represents cola drink as a substance category; used as closest parent for Coca-Cola Original Taste.

main_ingredients: high fructose corn syrup or sucrose

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Coca-Cola Original Taste, equivalent to approximately 346 g, is the typical single-serve portion sold in Singapore and convenience outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, cola, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174929)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry 174929 for carbonated cola beverage containing caffeine is an exact category match for Coca-Cola Original Taste. Values per 100 g are used directly. Branded product label values (Coca-Cola Company) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy figures and were cross-checked.

gi_source.title: Coca-Cola, carbonated soft drink — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, 2008, Diabetes Care)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for Coca-Cola carbonated soft drink is reported directly in the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 36.7) / 100 ≈ 23.1, rounded to 23.0. Serving carbohydrate based on 346 g × 10.6 g/100 g = 36.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (4 mg per 100 g); however, high sugar load may contribute to weight gain and secondary blood pressure elevation with habitual consumption.
hypertension.guidance: Coca-Cola Original Taste is very low in sodium and poses minimal direct hypertensive risk per serving, but individuals with hypertension should limit intake due to its high free-sugar content, which may promote weight gain and indirectly worsen blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free-sugar content (10.6 g per 100 g; approximately 36.7 g per 330 ml can) with a glycemic index of 63 and glycemic load of approximately 23 per serving, causing rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Coca-Cola Original Taste, as its high free-sugar content and medium-to-high glycemic load (GL ≈ 23 per can) produce rapid and significant postprandial blood glucose spikes inconsistent with glycaemic management targets.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar intake from regular consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral adiposity, and elevated cardiovascular risk; the beverage contains no saturated fat or cholesterol.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of Coca-Cola Original Taste contributes substantial free sugars (approximately 36.7 g per 330 ml can), which is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or eliminate intake in line with WHO free-sugar guidelines (less than 10% of total energy intake).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola Original Taste is the flagship carbonated cola soft drink produced by The Coca-Cola Company, originally formulated in the United States in 1886. It is a clear, dark-brown, highly carbonated beverage with a distinctive sweet, slightly acidic flavour profile derived from a proprietary blend of natural flavours, caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, and caffeine dissolved in carbonated water sweetened with sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup depending on the market. In Singapore, it is widely available in restaurants, convenience stores, and food delivery platforms including field survey, where it is commonly served as a beverage accompaniment to Japanese, Western, and local cuisine meals. The beverage contains approximately 41 kcal per 100 ml, with all energy derived from 10.6 g of free sugars per 100 ml; it contains no protein, no fat, no dietary fibre, and negligible micronutrients. A standard 330 ml can delivers approximately 139 kcal and 36.7 g of free sugars. From a clinical nutrition perspective, Coca-Cola Original Taste is a high-sugar, ultra-processed beverage with a glycemic index of 63 and a glycemic load of approximately 23 per 330 ml serving, making it a significant concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, obesity, metabolic syndrome, and cardiovascular disease. It is not recommended as part of a health-promoting dietary pattern and should be consumed only occasionally and in limited quantities. The beverage is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, and is free from common allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Coca-Cola?
What is the sugar content of Coke?
What is the glycemic index of Coca-Cola?
Can I drink Coke if I have high blood pressure?
How much sugar is in a 330ml can of Coca-Cola?
What are the ingredients in Coca-Cola Original Taste?
Is Coke bad for cardiovascular disease?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174929) for carbonated cola beverage, which is an exact category match for Coca-Cola Original Taste. GI of 63 sourced directly from the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson et al. (2008) in Diabetes Care. Branded product label values are consistent with USDA figures. All core macros and secondary nutrients are supported by approved primary sources.
notes: Protein_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174929); Coca-Cola Original Taste contains no protein by product formulation. Fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; this beverage contains no fat by product formulation. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all 0.0: confirmed zero by formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero; carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero by formulation. All vitamin values (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1 through vitamin_b12, folate, vitamin_d, vitamin_e) are 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; Coca-Cola Original Taste is not fortified and contains no meaningful vitamins. Glycemic index set to 63 (medium category) per University of Sydney GI Database; glycemic load per 330 ml serving calculated as (63 × 36.7) / 100 ≈ 23.0. The cuisine tag 'Japanese' in the input record reflects the restaurant context (Sukiya - Funan, a Japanese beef bowl chain) rather than the product origin; the beverage itself is American in origin and has been reclassified accordingly. Input cuisine_tags (Beef Dish, Japanese Curry, Grilled Chicken Skewers) are restaurant menu tags and are not applicable to this beverage product. Price data (SGD 3.80) preserved from input as contextual metadata but not included in the RESULT template fields. Serving size of 346 g is based on 330 ml can with density approximated at 1.048 g/ml consistent with USDA SR Legacy water content and sugar density for cola beverages.

improvement_actions_taken: Reclassified cuisine from 'Japanese' (restaurant context) to 'Global / American' (product origin) with explanation in notes.
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for protein_g=0.0 and fat_g=0.0 per zero-macro justification rule.
Set glycemic_index=63 (numeric >0) and gi_category='medium' from University of Sydney GI Database; calculated glycemic_load_per_serving=23.0.
Added Japanese local name (コカ・コーラ オリジナルテイスト) and Chinese names to satisfy multilingual requirements for the Japanese restaurant serving context.
Used only permitted ontology match_type values: 'closest_parent' for all three ontology systems.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent — all language-specific entries appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
Documented all 0.0 vitamin and micronutrient values with 'confirmed zero' justification in notes.
Ensured all per_100g values are numeric with no nulls, no strings, and no unsupported -1 values.
